rule_description: APP1 should be approximately twice APP2 at all throttle positions
TPS should track APP1 with less than 5% deviation
MAF increases proportionally with RPM above idle; MAF near 0 at RPM above 800 indicates fault
MAP decreases (more vacuum) as RPM increases at steady throttle; high MAP at idle indicates leak
TPS uses 5V_REF_A reference; TPS reads near 0 when vref is low even if physical sensor is OK
APP1 uses 5V_REF_A reference; APP1 reads near 0 when vref is low
APP2 uses 5V_REF_B reference; APP2 reads near 0 when vref is low
MAP uses 5V_REF_A reference; MAP reads zero or near-zero when vref is low
MAF uses 5V_REF_B reference; MAF reads zero when vref B is low
Upstream O2 voltage drives short-term fuel trim; lean O2 increases STFT; rich O2 decreases STFT
STFT and LTFT track together under persistent lean or rich condition; large combined value indicates system fault
